Home | News

Claude Opus 4 System Card

This document describes the safety evaluation and risk assessment for Claude Opus 4.

Overview

Claude Opus 4 is Anthropic's most capable model. It was trained using Constitutional AI and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). The model is available via the Anthropic API under the model identifier claude-opus-4.

Capabilities

Claude Opus 4 excels at complex reasoning, long-form code generation, and nuanced analysis tasks. It supports a 200K token context window and tool use, including computer use (beta). It is available in Claude.ai and the Anthropic API.

Safety Evaluations

We conducted extensive red-teaming and safety evaluations before releasing Claude Opus 4. The model demonstrates reduced rates of harmful output compared to prior versions. Automated alignment audit results show lower misaligned-behavior rates on our internal suite than Opus 3.7. Dangerous capability evaluations (DCE) for cyber, CBRN, and autonomy are provided below.

Limitations

The model may generate plausible-sounding but factually incorrect information (hallucination). It should not be used for high-stakes medical or legal decisions without human review. Known failure modes include over-refusal on sensitive topics and difficulty with very long exact-match retrieval.

Benchmarks and Evaluations

On GPQA (Diamond), Claude Opus 4 achieves 78.4%. On HumanEval, it achieves 93.2%. On MMLU-Pro, it achieves 82.1%. These are vendor-reported results. Independent third-party corroboration is pending at the time of this system card.

Training Data

The model was trained on a diverse dataset of internet text, books, and code with a knowledge cutoff of early 2026. We applied data filtering to remove personally identifiable information and known harmful content from training data.

Deployment and Access

Claude Opus 4 is available to Claude Pro, Team, and Enterprise customers at claude.ai, and via the Anthropic API at api.anthropic.com. Rate limits and context window details are available in the Anthropic API documentation.
